comment_text.sn35.96:1.1: This topic is addressed in several discourses, including AN 6.62 and AN 10.55. A stream-enterer is not liable to decline (AN 6.97).

root_text.sn35.96:1.1: “Parihānadhammañca vo, bhikkhave, desessāmi aparihānadhammañca cha ca abhibhāyatanāni.
root_text.sn35.96:1.4: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā uppajjanti pāpakā akusalā sarasaṅkappā saṁyojaniyā.
root_text.sn35.96:1.5: Tañce bhikkhu adhivāseti nappajahati na vinodeti na byantīkaroti na anabhāvaṁ gameti, veditabbametaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā:
root_text.sn35.96:2.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno jivhāya rasaṁ sāyitvā uppajjanti …pe…
root_text.sn35.96:2.2: puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno manasā dhammaṁ viññāya uppajjanti pāpakā akusalā sarasaṅkappā saṁyojaniyā.
root_text.sn35.96:2.3: Tañce bhikkhu adhivāseti nappajahati na vinodeti na byantīkaroti na anabhāvaṁ gameti, veditabbametaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā:
root_text.sn35.96:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā uppajjanti pāpakā akusalā sarasaṅkappā saṁyojaniyā.
root_text.sn35.96:3.3: Tañce bhikkhu nādhivāseti pajahati vinodeti byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti, veditabbametaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā:
root_text.sn35.96:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno jivhāya rasaṁ sāyitvā uppajjanti …pe…
root_text.sn35.96:4.2: puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno manasā dhammaṁ viññāya uppajjanti pāpakā akusalā sarasaṅkappā saṁyojaniyā.
root_text.sn35.96:4.3: Tañce bhikkhu nādhivāseti pajahati vinodeti byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti, veditabbametaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā:
root_text.sn35.96:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā nuppajjanti pāpakā akusalā sarasaṅkappā saṁyojaniyā.
root_text.sn35.96:5.6: puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno manasā dhammaṁ viññāya nuppajjanti pāpakā akusalā dhammā sarasaṅkappā saṁyojaniyā.

translation_text.sn35.96:1.1: “Mendicants, I will teach you who is liable to decline, who is not liable to decline, and the six fields of mastery.
translation_text.sn35.96:1.3: And how is someone liable to decline?
translation_text.sn35.96:1.4: When a mendicant sees a sight with the eye, bad, unskillful phenomena arise: memories and thoughts that tighten fetters.
translation_text.sn35.96:1.5: Suppose that mendicant tolerates them and doesn’t give them up, get rid of them, eliminate them, and obliterate them. They should understand:
translation_text.sn35.96:1.7: For this is what the Buddha calls decline.’
translation_text.sn35.96:2.1: Furthermore, when a mendicant hears a sound … smells an odor … tastes a flavor … feels a touch …
translation_text.sn35.96:2.2: knows an idea with the mind, bad, unskillful phenomena arise: memories and thoughts that tighten fetters.
translation_text.sn35.96:2.3: If that mendicant tolerates them and doesn’t give them up, get rid of them, eliminate them, and obliterate them, they should understand:
translation_text.sn35.96:2.5: For this is what the Buddha calls decline.’
translation_text.sn35.96:2.6: That’s how someone is liable to decline.
translation_text.sn35.96:3.1: And how is someone not liable to decline?
translation_text.sn35.96:3.2: When a mendicant sees a sight with the eye, bad, unskillful phenomena arise: memories and thoughts that tighten fetters.
translation_text.sn35.96:3.3: Suppose that mendicant doesn’t tolerate them but gives them up, gets rid of them, eliminates them, and obliterates them. They should understand:
translation_text.sn35.96:3.5: For this is what the Buddha calls non-decline.’
translation_text.sn35.96:4.1: Furthermore, when a mendicant hears a sound … smells an odor … tastes a flavor … feels a touch …
translation_text.sn35.96:4.2: knows an idea with the mind, bad, unskillful phenomena arise: memories and thoughts that tighten fetters.
translation_text.sn35.96:4.3: Suppose that mendicant doesn’t tolerate them but gives them up, gets rid of them, eliminates them, and obliterates them. They should understand:
translation_text.sn35.96:4.5: For this is what the Buddha calls non-decline.’
translation_text.sn35.96:4.6: That’s how someone is not liable to decline.
translation_text.sn35.96:5.1: And what are the six fields of mastery?
translation_text.sn35.96:5.2: When a mendicant sees a sight with the eye, bad, unskillful phenomena don’t arise: memories and thoughts that tighten fetters.
translation_text.sn35.96:5.5: For this is what the Buddha calls a field of mastery.’ …
translation_text.sn35.96:5.6: Furthermore, when a mendicant knows an idea with the mind, bad, unskillful phenomena don’t arise: memories and thoughts that tighten fetters.
translation_text.sn35.96:5.9: For this is what the Buddha calls a field of mastery.’
translation_text.sn35.96:5.10: These are the six fields of mastery.”

variant_text.sn35.96:1.4: akusalā sarasaṅkappā → akusalā dhammā sarasaṅkappā (sya-all, km, pts1ed, mr)
variant_text.sn35.96:1.5: byantīkaroti → vyantī karoti (bj); vyantikaroti (pts1ed); byantiṁ karoti (mr)
